title: Free from the law—oh, happy condition
firstLine: Free from the law—oh, happy condition!
publicDomainBasis: Philip Paul Bliss (1838–1876), Philip Paul Bliss (1838–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Free from the law—oh, happy condition!
Jesus hath bled, and there is remission;
Cursed by the law and bruised by the fall,
Christ hath redeemed us once for all.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
There on the cross your burden upbearing,
Thorns on His brow your Savior is wearing;
Never again your sin need appall,
You have been pardoned once for all.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Now we are free—there’s no condemnation;
Jesus provides a perfect salvation:
“Come unto Me,” oh, hear His sweet call,
Come, and He saves us once for all.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Children of God—oh, glorious calling,
Surely His grace will keep us from falling;
Passing from death to life at His call,
Blessed salvation once for all.
